stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Roxanne Swentzell is a Santa Clara Pueblo sculptor, ceramicist, and installation artist.
  • Many of her sculptures feature Pueblo women.
  • She depicts this subject matter using rounded clay forms with exaggerated facial expressions, developing a distinctive style that has influenced many other artists.
  • Her well-known sculpture Mud Woman Rolls On was installed at the Denver Art Museum.
stem: The student wants to describe the distinctive style of Swentzell’s sculptures. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: A sculptor, ceramicist, and installation artist, Swentzell developed a distinctive style for portraying Pueblo women.
The sculpture Mud Woman Rolls On is a well-known example of Swentzell’s style.
Many other artists have been influenced by the style of Swentzell’s sculptures.
Swentzell’s sculptures use rounded clay forms with exaggerated facial expressions to depict Pueblo women.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. It describes the distinctive style of Swentzell’s sculptures: rounded clay forms with exaggerated facial expressions depicting Pueblo women.

Choice A mentions that Swentzell developed a distinctive style but doesn’t describe it. Choice B names an example of her style without describing it. Choice C reports the style’s influence without describing it.
